intent.primary_goal: Describe the CobbleWright utils layer as a collection of helper modules so consuming agents can reason about where to find reusable, low-level functionality.

identity.role: A utility capsule for CobbleWright's shared helper functions and classes.

priorities: keep utility functions pure and stateless where possible
group related helpers into single files (e.g., 'vector-helpers.js', 'block-helpers.js')
document function signatures and purposes clearly with JSDoc comments
avoid dependencies on bot or sharedState; utilities should be self-contained

never_do: add complex, high-level application logic that belongs in a plugin
access the Minecraft bot instance directly

folder_scope.purpose: Shared, reusable helper functions for the CobbleWright runtime and plugins.

architecture.loader_behavior: Utility modules are not auto-loaded like plugins. They are explicitly required by `architect.js` or other plugins.
architecture.usage_pattern: Modules export functions or classes that can be imported and used by any other part of the system.
architecture.extension_style: The folder is designed for adding new, self-contained helper modules.

utility_catalog.object-helpers.js: Provides safe object property access utilities, like a `get` function to prevent errors from nested property lookups on null/undefined objects.
utility_catalog.player-helpers.js: Provides helper functions for working with player objects, such as finding the most recently active player.
utility_catalog.sc-capsules.js: Contains helper functions for loading, parsing, merging, and normalizing Semantic Capsules (.sc.json files).
utility_catalog.schematic-importer.js: A utility for importing and converting `.schematic` files into a blueprint format the bot can understand.
utility_catalog.text-helpers.js: A collection of stateless functions for text formatting, such as escaping characters for use in Minecraft commands.

runtime_guidance.for_prompts: Use this capsule when reasoning about where to find or create low-level, reusable helper functions.
runtime_guidance.for_drafting: When creating a new utility, add it to the catalog here with a brief description of its purpose.
runtime_guidance.for_maintenance: Keep the catalog in sync with the files in the utils/ folder.

trust.note: High-confidence summary. The catalog has been reconciled with the actual files in the directory.
